operating_state: Revenue reached $26.0 billion, up 262% year-over-year and 18% sequentially, driven by Data Center growth of 427% from higher Hopper GPU shipments for AI training and inference applications. Networking revenue grew 242% on InfiniBand solutions strength while Gaming increased 18% despite sequential decline from seasonal laptop weakness. Customer concentration remains elevated with four customers each representing over 10% of total revenue through direct and indirect channels.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded to 78.4% from 64.6% prior year, primarily driven by strong Data Center revenue growth and favorable product mix, offset by $279 million in net inventory provisions creating 1.1% unfavorable impact. Operating expenses increased 39% year-over-year driven by compensation increases and compute infrastructure investments. Supply chain complexity increased through expanded vendor relationships and prepaid manufacturing agreements while operational restructuring costs emerged from transitioning operations out of China and Hong Kong.
material_events: Executed ten-for-one forward stock split effective June 7, 2024, increasing authorized common stock to 80 billion shares. Quarterly dividend increased 150% to $0.10 per share pre-split with $8.0 billion in share repurchases completed and $14.5 billion remaining authorized. Blackwell GPU architecture entered production with customer samples shipping and volume shipments expected in second half of fiscal year.
macro_exposures: Export control restrictions significantly reduced China Data Center revenue with no licenses received for restricted products, prompting development of China-specific compliant offerings. Supply chain remains concentrated in Asia-Pacific with potential vulnerability to expanded export controls and geopolitical tensions. Israel-Hamas conflict affects approximately 4,000 employees supporting networking development with extended military duty creating operational disruption while regulatory scrutiny intensifies globally with ongoing inquiries regarding GPU sales and AI partnerships.
forward_outlook: Blackwell demand expected to exceed projected supply availability through fiscal 2025 with supply constraints continuing into next fiscal year and customer shipments ramping in second half. Management plans broader and faster Data Center product launch cadence to address diverse AI opportunities while managing increased transition complexity from accelerated product cycles.
uncertainty_profile: Product transition complexity increasing with simultaneous shipping of multiple architectures and accelerated launch cadence creating supply-demand management challenges and qualification timing risks. Demand forecasting complexity persists across multiple product transitions and emerging AI use cases with cryptocurrency mining and gray market resales creating additional volatility. Export control scope may expand while regulatory scrutiny around AI technologies and competitive practices intensifies.
